Cape Coral_FL.script: Nobody talks about this when they're shopping new construction in Cape Coral — but no HOA, no CDD fees, and a corner lot on a freshwater canal changes your monthly number more than most buyers realize. This specific home is 4 beds, 3 baths, nearly 2,000 square feet, 3-car garage, pool with cage, outdoor kitchen, impact windows — brand new — and because it's not tucked inside a community, you're keeping an extra $300 to $600 a month that most buyers in this market are just handing over. I've watched buyers overlook that math over and over again, and it's usually a $5,000-plus mistake by the end of year one. Freshwater canal also means no salt damage eating your dock and no saltwater flood zone premiums stacking on top of everything else. So tell me — are you budgeting with or without HOA fees right now? Drop your number below and I'll show you what's actually available this week.

Cape Coral_FL.title: Florida's housing market is crashing in 2026. Sellers are ...
Cape Coral_FL.caption: Florida's housing market is crashing in 2026. Sellers are losing money. Prices are down as much as $30,000, $40,000 and even $100,000 in certain neighborhoods. This house is down almost 10% from its 2022 price, and one has to wonder how much more the values could decline. Florida is a boom-or-bust housing market, and there's big long-term growth. But right not there's a bust occurring. Anyone buying in Florida in 2026 needs to understand the 12-month price forecast and overvaluation rate for their area. Access by downloading the Reventure Mobile App or head to www.reventure.app.
Cape Coral_FL.script: Here's something nobody selling Cape Coral homes wants you to know right now. Some sellers in this market are down $30,000, $40,000 — I've seen one neighborhood where a seller took a $100,000 loss from their 2022 peak. That's not a typo. The Pine Island corridor, parts of Burnt Store, even some newer builds off Chiquita — they're all showing price drops of 8 to 12 percent year over year. Now here's the thing — Cape Coral has massive long-term upside, and I actually believe in this market deeply. But buying blind in 2026 without knowing your specific zip code's 12-month forecast could cost you serious money before the rebound hits. I pulled the overvaluation data for six Cape Coral neighborhoods this week, and the difference between them is shocking. What street or neighborhood are you looking at? Drop it below and I'll tell you exactly where it stands right now.

Cape Coral_FL.script: Stop scrolling — this is the part of Cape Coral nobody tells you about until it's too late.
